headline: Chip bull market cracking — traders seek protection
detail: Wall Street is openly questioning whether the semiconductor rally is losing steam. This hits your book directly — AVGO, SMH, CRWD, and PLTR are all chip-adjacent or high-beta tech names. On margin, a sector rotation out of chips would compress your portfolio faster than a cash account.

headline: Everyone's buying the dip — that's the warning sign
detail: When dip-buying becomes consensus, it often signals late-cycle behavior where one bad catalyst can cause a crowded unwind. Your portfolio is ~90% concentrated in exactly the names these crowded longs own. If sentiment flips, high-beta tech names like PLTR and CRWD tend to fall hardest and fastest.

headline: Index funds hiding looming tech-stock risk
detail: The warning here is that passive index exposure masks how top-heavy and volatile tech concentration has become. Your QQQ and SPY positions look stable on paper, but they're loaded with the same names you're already concentrated in, meaning your real tech exposure is even larger than it appears.

headline: New bull market leaders far more volatile than old guard
detail: The article flags that today's leading tech stocks carry significantly higher volatility profiles than prior market leaders. PLTR, CRWD, and SNOW all fit this description — great when momentum is up, brutal on margin when it reverses. SNOW is your best cushion at +48%, but protecting that profit matters now.
